title: Heatwave Warnings Issued Across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana - The Hans India
full_text:
Heatwave Warnings Issued Across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana
Andhra Pradesh: As temperatures soar across the state, the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority has issued urgent warnings, advising people to avoid outdoor activities between 12:00 PM and 4:00 PM. Record-high temperatures and intensifying heatwaves have led authorities to urge vigilance, with the scorching sun already affecting daily life from as early as 8:00 AM.
The Meteorological Department predicts that on 14 April, several districts in Rayalaseema and Coastal Andhra will experience severe heatwave conditions. In particular, 33 mandals across Eluru, Polavaram, Vizianagaram, Srikakulam, Parvathipuram Manyam, and Alluri Sitarama Raju districts are expected to face extreme heat, with moderate conditions in another 44 mandals. Specific mandals in Polavaram, Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, and Parvathipuram Manyam districts are forecast to endure the worst of the heat.
On Monday, Kadapa recorded a maximum temperature of 44.4°C, with Sanjamala in Nandyal district reaching 44°C. Durgi in Palnadu district and Nuliveedu in Annamayya district registered 42.8°C and 42°C respectively. The Meteorological Department advised residents to wear hats or carry umbrellas when outdoors and to stay well-hydrated, emphasising the increased risk of heatstroke.
Meanwhile, in Telangana, the Hyderabad Meteorological Department warned that the heatwave is likely to continue throughout the week, with temperatures expected to rise by 2 to 3 degrees. On Monday, four fatalities due to heatstroke were reported in the state. Nizamabad recorded a maximum temperature of 43.2°C, while Hyderabad’s temperature exceeded 40°C. City streets are largely deserted during the afternoons, as residents avoid the intense heat, which is expected to persist for several more days.

title: Heat Wave Alert in Telangana as Temperatures Set to Rise Over Next 5 Days - Deccan Chronicle

full_text:
14 April 2026 11:35 AM IST14 April 2026 11:35 AM IST
IMD forecasts dry weather across state; heat wave conditions likely in isolated areas from April 15 onwards
x
Women cover their heads with shawls to shield themselves from the heat.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast dry weather conditions across Telangana for the next five days, with temperatures expected to rise gradually.
According to the latest seven-day forecast issued from the Meteorological Centre in Hyderabad on April 14, maximum temperatures are likely to increase by 2–3°C over the next three days.
While no warnings have been issued for Day 1, the IMD has cautioned that heat wave conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of the state from Day 2 (April 15) through Day 5 (April 19).
Officials have advised residents, especially farmers and those exposed to outdoor conditions, to take necessary precautions to cope with rising temperatures and potential heat wave impacts.

title: Telangana heatwave intensifies; officials urge caution - Hyderabad Mail

full_text:
Telangana : Telangana is witnessing a sharp rise in heatwave conditions, with temperatures crossing 42°C in several districts, according to the Hyderabad Meteorological Department.
The intensity of the heat has increased over the past two days, with strong hot winds adding to discomfort. Even indoors, elderly people, children, pregnant women and those with chronic illnesses are facing difficulties.
Officials said the heatwave is likely to persist for a few more days.
Hot winds raise health risks, especially for outdoor workers
The Meteorological Department has urged people to remain alert and avoid stepping out unless necessary. Those who must go outdoors have been advised to take precautions.
Authorities warned that the intensity of hot winds poses serious health risks, particularly for small traders and outdoor workers. They advised frequent intake of drinking water to prevent dehydration.
Overall, officials said “danger conditions” are likely to continue in Telangana for the next few days.

title: Telangana sizzles as temperatures cross 43°C; all 33 districts record above 41°C - The Hindu
full_text:
Telangana is in the grip of an intense heatwave, with maximum temperatures crossing 43°C in two districts and remaining above 41°C across all districts on Monday (April 13, 2026). Data from the Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) showed that 22 districts recorded temperatures above 42°C, while the remaining 11 districts saw temperatures between 41°C and 42°C. The lowest maximum temperature recorded anywhere in the State was 41°C in Hyderabad.
Aloor in Nizamabad district emerged as the hottest location in the State, recording a maximum temperature of 43.2°C. This was closely followed by Gudipally in Nalgonda district at 43.1°C. In Hyderabad, Nampally recorded a maximum temperature of 41°C.
Published - April 14, 2026 12:45 pm IST

title: Hyderabad heatwave raises risk of heatstroke, diarrhoeal diseases - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: The two months of severe summer in Hyderabad are upon us, and with it comes the danger of falling sick with summer-related ailments.
As temperatures consistently climb to 40 degree Celsius and above, the risk of heat strokes and dehydration becomes a daily reality for not only people in Hyderabad but also in districts.
Already, the local clinics, nursing homes, and small-to-medium hospitals, as well as major tertiary care centres, including Fever Hospital, have gradually started registering a significant change in the profile of patients arriving for treatment.
Commenting on the present scenario, District public health officials in Hyderabad said "Weather is changing significantly but the body needs to acclimatise. By evening, in Hyderabad, we are witnessing thunderstorms and cool but gusty winds. By afternoon, the temperatures are touching 40 degree Celsius. Due to these changes, Area Hospital and private health care facilities will witness both heat-related exhaustion and water-borne infections".
The fluctuating weather conditions create a breeding ground for ailments like acute diarrhoeal disease and dehydration as the extreme heat causes food to spoil faster and residents struggle to maintain consistent hydration, doctors said. Vulnerable groups, particularly children and those with pre-existing conditions, remain at the highest risk during this transition period.
In addition to the threat of heatstroke, there is a marked rise in acute diarrheal disease (ADD) linked to the consumption of contaminated water and food that spoils quickly in the rising heat.
Senior doctors in Hyderabad have urged families to focus on vulnerable groups in the family, including infants, young children, pregnant women, and individuals with underlying conditions like heart disease or high blood pressure, who must be particularly vigilant during these peak months.

title: మండే ఎండలు! - Andhrajyothy
search_term: ("వడ గాలులు" OR "ఎండ వేడి" OR "భారీ ఎండలు" OR "హీట్ వేవ్" OR "ఉష్ణ తరంగం" OR "కడ ఎండలు" OR మండుటెండ OR "ఎండల తీవ్రత" OR "అసాధారణ ఎండలు" OR "తీవ్ర ఎండలు") Telangana
full_text:
మండే ఎండలు!
రోహిణి కార్తె రాకమునుపే రోకళ్లు పగిలేలా ఎండ దంచికొడుతోంది. ఉదయం 10 నుంచి సాయంత్రం నాలుగింటిదాకా మాడు పగిలేలా ఎండ కొడుతోంది.
రాష్ట్రంలో భానుడి భగభగ, ఉక్కబోత
నేటి నుంచి వారం పాటు భారీ ఎండలు
హైదరాబాద్, ఏప్రిల్ 13 (ఆంధ్రజ్యోతి): రోహిణి కార్తె రాకమునుపే రోకళ్లు పగిలేలా ఎండ దంచికొడుతోంది. ఉదయం 10 నుంచి సాయంత్రం నాలుగింటిదాకా మాడు పగిలేలా ఎండ కొడుతోంది. రాష్ట్రంపై భానుడి నిప్పులు చిమ్ముతున్నాడు. నేటి నుంచి వారం పాటు ఎండ తీవ్రత మరింత పెరగనుంది. అన్ని జిల్లాల్లో పగటిపూట ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు 42-45 డిగ్రీల మధ్య నమోదయ్యే అవకాశం ఉందని హైదరాబాద్ వాతావరణ కేంద్రం హెచ్చరించింది. ఉష్ణోగ్రతలకు సంబంధించి అన్ని జిల్లాలకు ఆరెంజ్ అలెర్ట్ జారీ చేసింది. ప్రధానంగా ఆదిలాబాద్, ఆసిఫాబాద్, నిర్మల్, నిజామాబాద్, జగిత్యాల, మంచిర్యాల, కరీంనగర్, భూపాలపల్లి, ములుగు, నల్లగొండ, సూర్యాపేట, గద్వాల జిల్లాల్లో భారీగా ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు నమోదవుతాయని హెచ్చరించింది. మిగతా జిల్లాల్లోనూ 41-43 మధ్య నమోదయ్యే అవకాశం ఉందని పేర్కొంది. హైదరాబాద్లో 40-42 డిగ్రీల మధ్య ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు నమోదవుతాయని వెల్లడించింది. సోమవారం ఆదిలాబాద్ జిల్లా తలమడుగులో అత్యధికంగా 41.8 డిగ్రీల గరిష్ట ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు నమోదైనట్లు తెలిపింది.

title: ఎండ.. ప్రచండ సిటీలో 40.1 డిగ్రీలు - Namasthe Telangana
search_term: ("ఉష్ణోగ్రత పెరుగుదల" OR "డిగ్రీల సెల్సియస్" OR "పాదరసం పెరిగింది" OR "గరిష్ట ఉష్ణోగ్రత" OR "రికార్డు ఉష్ణోగ్రత") Telangana
full_text: సిటీబ్యూరో, ఏప్రిల్ 13 (నమస్తే తెలంగాణ): గ్రేటర్లో ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు ఠారెత్తిస్తున్నాయి. పగటి ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు 40 డిగ్రీలు దాటి నమోదవుతుండడంతో మధ్యాహ్నం సమయంలో జనం బయటకు రావడానికి జంకుతున్నారు. సోమవారం ఉదయం నుంచి రాత్రి వరకు నగరంలో గరిష్ఠం 40.1, కనిష్ఠం 26.9 డిగ్రీల సెల్సియస్, గాలిలో తేమ 35శాతంగా నమోదైనట్లు హైదరాబాద్ వాతావరణ కేంద్రం అధికారులు వెల్లడించారు. ఈ సంవత్సరం ఏప్రిల్ మాసంలో పగటి ఉష్ణోగ్రతలు 40 డిగ్రీలు దాటడం ఇదే తొలిసారి.
